response:
for each element in the array nums we need to compute the sum of absolute differences between that element and all other elements in the array with a twist for every third element. if the index i is a multiple of 3 we calculate the sum of the squares of the differences instead of the absolute differences. to achieve this efficiently we can leverage the sorted nature of the array and precompute prefix sums. this allows us to quickly calculate the sum of absolute differences for any index i by using the prefix sums of the elements before and after i. for the square of the differences we can iterate through the array and compute the sum directly since the array is small enough for this operation.

***

the approach involves first computing the prefix sum array which helps in determining the sum of absolute differences efficiently. for each index i we can split the array into two parts the elements before i and the elements after i. using the prefix sum array we can compute the sum of absolute differences in constant time for each index. for indices that are multiples of 3 we calculate the sum of squared differences by iterating through the array and adding the square of the difference between nums[i] and each other element. this ensures that we meet the time constraints while handling both parts of the problem correctly. the final result array is built by combining these computed values for each index.
